text:
Market today: Tech under mild pressure as Meta cloud-pivot headlines spook AI infrastructure names. The real driver is rotation - money moving away from pure-play AI compute toward defensive/value names. Your chip-heavy, leveraged book is in the crosshairs.

Your holdings: SOXL (-14.52%) is the loudest problem - 3x levered chips, sitting just $17 above your $200 stop, and decay is eating you daily. Margin amplifies every down tick here. SPAL (-13.72%) is a brand-new, very volatile leveraged ETF - basically an IPO-level wildcard - and at $21.70 it's dangerously close to your $21.38 stop; one bad print triggers it. SMH (-2.5%) is softer too, confirming chips are the weak link today.

Watch: Whether SOXL holds $200 - a break there should be a hard exit, no negotiating. Also watch Meta cloud headlines for follow-through selling into SMH and chip names.

Bottom line: Protect capital in your leveraged positions before anything else - margin turns a bad day into a crisis fast.
plays: SOXL: Stop at $200 is your line - if price approaches, be ready to cut without hesitation. Leveraged decay plus margin is a dangerous combo, no reason to let this run further against you.
SPAL: At $21.70 with a stop at $21.38, you have almost no room. Consider whether this position size makes sense on margin - a gap down could blow through your stop.
No new buys today - chip rotation is working against your book and adding leverage here would be fighting the tape.
